events.title: Friday (Jumu'ah)
events.shortStory: Friday is the best day on which the sun rises — the day Adam was created, admitted to Paradise, and removed from it. The Prophet ﷺ said it is the master of days, containing an hour in which supplication is answered.

quranRefs.text: O you who have believed, when the adhan is called for the prayer on the day of Jumu'ah, then proceed to the remembrance of Allah and leave trade. That is better for you, if you only knew.
And when the prayer has been concluded, disperse within the land and seek from the bounty of Allah, and remember Allah often that you may succeed.

hadithRefs.text: The best day on which the sun has risen is Friday; on it Adam was created, on it he was entered into Paradise, and on it he was expelled from it.

Whoever takes a bath on Friday, purifies himself as much as he can, then oils his hair or applies perfume, then proceeds to the mosque and does not separate two persons, then prays as much as Allah has written for him, then remains silent when the Imam speaks, his sins between that Friday and the next will be forgiven.
There is an hour on Friday when a Muslim, while standing in prayer, asks Allah for something, and Allah gives it to him.

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَعَلَى آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ
duas.transliteration: Allahumma salli ala Muhammad wa ala aali Muhammad
duas.translation: O Allah, send blessings upon Muhammad and the family of Muhammad

events.title: Islamic New Year (Hijri)
events.shortStory: The Hijri calendar was instituted by Umar ibn al-Khattab (RA) in 638 CE, beginning from the Prophet's migration (Hijrah) from Makkah to Madinah — the event that transformed a persecuted community into a society built on faith. The Hijrah was not merely a physical relocation but a spiritual turning point: leaving behind all that displeases Allah for all that pleases Him. Each new year invites the believer to renew that inward migration, taking account of the soul as Umar (RA) famously did of himself before the day's end.

quranRefs.text: If you do not aid him, Allah has already aided him when those who disbelieved had driven him out, and the second of two, when they were in the cave, and he said to his companion: Do not grieve; indeed Allah is with us.

Indeed, the number of months with Allah is twelve months in the register of Allah from the day He created the heavens and the earth; of these, four are sacred. That is the correct religion, so do not wrong yourselves during them.

hadithRefs.text: Actions are but by intentions, and every man shall have only that which he intended. Thus whoever migrated for Allah and His Messenger, his migration was for Allah and His Messenger.
The Prophet ﷺ said: The year is twelve months, of which four are sacred: Dhul-Qa'dah, Dhul-Hijjah, Muharram, and Rajab of Mudar.

duas.arabic: رَبَّنَا آتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
duas.transliteration: Rabbana atina fid-dunya hasanatan wa fil-akhirati hasanatan wa qina 'adhaban-nar
duas.translation: Our Lord, give us in this world good and in the Hereafter good, and protect us from the punishment of the Fire.

duas.transliteration: Rabbana la tu'akhidhna in nasina ak akhta'na
duas.translation: Our Lord, do not impose blame upon us if we forget or err.

events.title: Day of Tasu'a
events.shortStory: The Prophet ﷺ intended to fast the 9th of Muharram alongside the 10th to distinguish the Muslim practice from the People of the Book. When he was told that the Jews fasted the 10th alone, he declared: If I live until next year, I will surely fast the 9th. Fasting Tasu'a together with Ashura is therefore a recommended sunnah that honors the Prophet's wish to differ from previous nations in devotion.

quranRefs.text: O you who have believed, decreed upon you is fasting as it was decreed upon those before you, that you may become righteous.

hadithRefs.text: When the Prophet ﷺ fasted the day of Ashura and ordered it to be fasted, they said: O Messenger of Allah, it is a day the Jews and Christians venerate. He said: Then next year, if Allah wills, we will fast the ninth.
The Prophet ﷺ was asked about fasting on the day of Ashura, and he said: It expiates the sins of the previous year.

duas.arabic: رَبَّنَا ظَلَمْنَا أَنفُسَنَا وَإِن لَّمْ تَغْفِرْ لَنَا وَتَرْحَمْنَا لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ
duas.transliteration: Rabbana zalamna anfusana wa in lam taghfir lana wa tarhamna lanakunanna minal-khasirin
duas.translation: Our Lord, we have wronged ourselves, and if You do not forgive us and have mercy upon us, we will surely be among the losers.

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِي ذَنْبِي كُلَّهُ، دِقَّهُ وَجِلَّهُ، وَأَوَّلَهُ وَآخِرَهُ، وَعَلَانِيَتَهُ وَسِرَّهُ
duas.transliteration: Allahummaghfir li dhanbi kullahu, diqqahu wa jillahu, wa awwalahu wa akhirahu, wa 'alaniyatahu wa sirrahu
duas.translation: O Allah, forgive me all my sins — the small and the great, the first and the last, the open and the hidden.

events.title: Day of Ashura
events.shortStory: On this day Allah saved Musa (AS) and the Children of Israel from Pharaoh by parting the sea, drowning the oppressor. When the Prophet ﷺ arrived in Madinah, he found the Jews fasting this day and said, 'We have more right to Musa than you,' and he fasted it and ordered others to fast.

quranRefs.text: And We took the Children of Israel across the sea, and Pharaoh and his soldiers pursued them in tyranny and enmity until, when drowning overtook him, he said, 'I believe there is no deity except that in whom the Children of Israel believe, and I am of the Muslims.'
And We had inspired to Moses, 'Travel by night with My servants and strike for them a dry path through the sea; you will not fear being overtaken nor be afraid.'

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ came to Madinah and found the Jews fasting the day of Ashura. He said: 'We have more right to Musa than you.' So he fasted it and ordered it to be fasted.
The Prophet ﷺ was asked about fasting on the day of Ashura and said: 'It expiates the sins of the previous year.'

duas.arabic: الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي نَجَّانَا مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ

events.title: Mawlid an-Nabi
events.shortStory: The Prophet Muhammad ﷺ was born in the Year of the Elephant (570 CE) in Makkah, a mercy to the worlds through whom Allah completed His message. His birth marked the dawn of a prophethood that would transform humanity, calling people from darkness into light through the worship of One God. Believers honor his birth by increasing love for him, sending abundant salawat, and renewing their commitment to follow his sunnah in daily life.

quranRefs.text: And We have not sent you except as a mercy to the worlds.

Certainly did Allah confer favor upon the believers when He sent among them a Messenger from themselves, reciting to them His verses and purifying them and teaching them the Book and wisdom, although they had been before in manifest error.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ said: I have been given superiority over the other prophets in six respects: I have been given words which are concise but comprehensive in meaning; I have been helped by terror (in the hearts of enemies); booty has been made lawful to me; the earth has been made for me a place of worship and ritually pure; I have been sent to all mankind; and the line of prophets is closed with me.
The Prophet ﷺ said: I am the closest of the people to Isa son of Maryam in this world and the Hereafter. The prophets are brothers; their mothers are different but their religion is one.

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ وَسَلِّمْ عَلَى نَبِيِّنَا مُحَمَّدٍ
duas.transliteration: Allahumma salli wa sallim 'ala nabiyyina Muhammad
duas.translation: O Allah, send blessings and peace upon our Prophet Muhammad.

duas.translation: Our Lord, we have believed, so record us among the witnesses.

events.title: Isra and Mi'raj
events.shortStory: In one miraculous night, Allah took His Prophet ﷺ from al-Masjid al-Haram to al-Masjid al-Aqsa, then ascended through the heavens where he met earlier prophets and received the gift of the five daily prayers — the mi'raj of the believers.

quranRefs.text: Exalted is He who took His Servant by night from al-Masjid al-Haram to al-Masjid al-Aqsa, whose surroundings We have blessed, to show him of Our signs. Indeed, He is the Hearing, the Seeing.
And he certainly saw him in another descent.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ said: I was brought al-Buraq, a white long beast, bigger than a donkey and smaller than a mule, which could place its hooves at the farthest boundary of its sight. I mounted it and went to Bayt al-Maqdis.
The five daily prayers were made obligatory on the night of the Isra. Allah made fifty prayers obligatory, then reduced them to five.

duas.arabic: سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي أَسْرَىٰ بِعَبْدِهِ لَيْلًا

events.title: Laylat al-Bara'at
events.shortStory: On the 15th night of Sha'ban, Allah's mercy descends; He forgives all except the idolater and the one who harbors hatred. It is a night of seeking forgiveness and turning back to Allah before the arrival of Ramadan. The Prophet ﷺ would spend it in prayer and visit the graveyard of Baqi' to supplicate for the deceased. It is a call to purify the heart, mend broken ties, and prepare the soul for the blessed month ahead.

quranRefs.text: Indeed, We sent it down during a blessed night. Indeed, We were to warn mankind.
Therein is decreed every matter of wisdom.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ said: Allah looks down on the middle night of Sha'ban and forgives all His creation, except for the one who associates partners with Him or the one who harbors enmity.
The Prophet ﷺ said: Allah descends to the lowest heaven on the middle night of Sha'ban and forgives all those in His creation except the idolater and the one who harbors hatred.

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ عَفُوٌّ كَرِيمٌ تُحِبُّ الْعَفْوَ فَاعْفُ عَنِّي

duas.translation: Our Lord, let not our hearts deviate after You have guided us, and grant us from Yourself mercy. Indeed, You are the Bestower.

events.title: First Day of Ramadan
events.shortStory: Ramadan is the month in which the Quran was revealed — a guidance for humanity. Allah prescribed fasting upon this Ummah as He did upon those before, that we may attain taqwa. The gates of Paradise open and the devils are chained.

quranRefs.text: The month of Ramadhan is that in which was revealed the Quran, a guidance for the people and clear proofs of guidance and criterion. So whoever sights the month, let him fast it.
O you who have believed, decreed upon you is fasting as it was decreed upon those before you that you may become righteous.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ said: When Ramadan comes, the gates of Paradise are opened, the gates of Hell are locked, and the devils are chained.
The Prophet ﷺ said: Whoever fasts Ramadan with faith and seeking reward, his previous sins will be forgiven.

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صُمْ لِي فِيهِ صِيَامَ الصَّائِمِينَ

events.title: Laylat al-Qadr (21st Night)
events.shortStory: The Night of Decree is better than a thousand months — worship on it equals 83 years. The Prophet ﷺ said to seek it in the odd last ten nights of Ramadan. It is the night the Quran was first revealed.

quranRefs.text: Indeed, We sent the Quran down during the Night of Decree.
The Night of Decree is better than a thousand months.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ said: Seek Laylat al-Qadr in the odd nights of the last ten days of Ramadan.
I said: O Messenger of Allah, if I know which night is Laylat al-Qadr, what should I say? He said: Say: Allahumma innaka 'afuwwun tuhibbul-'afwa fa'fu 'anni.

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ عَفُوٌّ تُحِبُّ الْعَفْوَ فَاعْفُ عَنِّي

duas.translation: O Allah, You are the Most Forgiving and You love to forgive, so forgive me

Peace it is until the emergence of dawn.

events.shortStory: The most widely cited candidate for Laylat al-Qadr, the night the Quran first descended to the lowest heaven. It is better than a thousand months; worship on it exceeds eighty years of devotion.

events.title: Eid al-Fitr
events.shortStory: The festival of breaking the fast, marking the end of Ramadan. The Prophet ﷺ would take a bath, eat dates before going out, lead the Eid prayer in the open, and the day is a reward from Allah for those who fasted.

quranRefs.text: ...Allah intends for you ease and does not intend for you hardship and [wants] for you to complete the period and to glorify Allah for that to which He has guided you; and perhaps you will be grateful.
And when the prayer has been concluded, disperse within the land and seek from the bounty of Allah, and remember Allah often that you may succeed.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ never proceeded on the day of Eid al-Fitr without eating some dates, and he used to eat an odd number of them.
The Prophet ﷺ ordered Zakat al-Fitr before the people went out for the Eid prayer.

duas.arabic: تَقَبَّلَ اللَّهُ مِنَّا وَمِنْكُمْ
duas.transliteration: Taqabbalallahu minna wa minkum
duas.translation: May Allah accept it from us and from you

duas.arabic: اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ وَلِلَّهِ الْحَمْدُ
duas.transliteration: Allahu Akbar Allahu Akbar, la ilaha illallah, wallahu Akbar Allahu Akbar wa lillahil-hamd
duas.translation: Allah is the Greatest, Allah is the Greatest. There is no god but Allah. Allah is the Greatest, Allah is the Greatest, and all praise is for Allah

sunnahActions.subtitle: Purify yourself before going to prayer

sunnahActions.title: Eat an odd number of dates
sunnahActions.subtitle: Before the Eid prayer, as the Prophet ﷺ did

sunnahActions.subtitle: Listen to the khutbah; take one route there and another back

events.title: Six Fasts of Shawwal
events.shortStory: Whoever fasts Ramadan and then follows it with six days of Shawwal, it is as if they fasted the entire year — for a good deed is multiplied by ten. The Prophet ﷺ encouraged these voluntary fasts after Eid, framing them as a means to extend the reward of Ramadan throughout the year. The fasts may be kept consecutively or spread across the month, and they protect the worshipper from losing the discipline built in Ramadan. They are a sign of acceptance of one's Ramadan and gratitude to Allah.

quranRefs.text: Whoever comes with a good deed will have ten times the like thereof, and whoever comes with an evil deed will not be recompensed except the like thereof; and they will not be wronged.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ said: Whoever fasts Ramadan and then follows it with six of Shawwal, it is as if he fasted the entire year.
The Prophet ﷺ said: The month of Ramadan is like ten months, and six days of Shawwal are like two months, so that is a complete year.

duas.arabic: رَبَّنَا تَقَبَّلْ مِنَّا ۖ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
duas.transliteration: Rabbana taqabbal minna innaka antas-Sami'ul-'Alim
duas.translation: Our Lord, accept it from us. Indeed, You are the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صُمْ لِي فِيهِ صِيَامَ الْقَائِمِينَ وَنَوْمِي فِيهِ نَوْمَ الصَّائِمِينَ
duas.transliteration: Allahumma sum li fih siyamal-qa'imina wa nawmi fih nawmas-sa'imina
duas.translation: O Allah, for me make my fast in it the fast of those who stand in worship, and my sleep the sleep of those who fast.

sunnahActions.title: Plan to fast six days
sunnahActions.subtitle: Schedule them across Shawwal; together with Ramadan equals a year

sunnahActions.title: Begin fasting today
sunnahActions.subtitle: Resume voluntary fasting after Eid day

sunnahActions.title: Thank Allah for Ramadan
sunnahActions.subtitle: Acknowledge the blessing of completed worship

sunnahActions.title: Reflect on the ten-fold reward
sunnahActions.subtitle: A single good deed is multiplied by ten — ponder Allah's generosity

events.title: First Ten Days of Dhul-Hijjah
events.shortStory: The Prophet ﷺ said there are no days in which righteous deeds are more beloved to Allah than these ten days. They combine every major act of worship — prayer, fasting, charity, and Hajj — and Allah swears by them in the Quran.

quranRefs.text: By the dawn.
And the ten nights.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ said: No good deeds are more beloved to Allah than those done in these first ten days of Dhul-Hijjah. They said: Not even jihad for the sake of Allah? He said: Not even jihad, except a man who goes out with his life and his wealth and returns with neither.
The Prophet ﷺ said: There are no days greater or more beloved to Allah for good deeds than these ten days, so increase in them: tahleel, takbeer, and tahmeed.

duas.arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَاللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ اللَّهُ أَكْبَرُ وَلِلَّهِ الْحَمْدُ
duas.transliteration: La ilaha illallah, wallahu Akbar Allahu Akbar wa lillahil-hamd
duas.translation: There is no god but Allah, Allah is the Greatest, Allah is the Greatest, and all praise is for Allah

duas.arabic: رَبَّنَا هَبْ لَنَا مِنْ أَزْوَاجِنَا وَذُرِّيَّاتِنَا قُرَّةَ أَعْيُنٍ
duas.transliteration: Rabbana hab lana min azwajina wa dhurriyatina qurrata a'yun
duas.translation: Our Lord, grant us from among our spouses and offspring comfort to our eyes

sunnahActions.title: Recite Takbeer frequently
sunnahActions.subtitle: Allahu Akbar, Allahu Akbar, la ilaha illallah... loud and often

sunnahActions.subtitle: Especially the Day of Arafah; deeds are multiplied here

sunnahActions.title: Increase all good deeds
sunnahActions.subtitle: Charity, dhikr, Quran — multiply worship these ten days

sunnahActions.title: Do not cut hair/nails (if sacrificing)
sunnahActions.subtitle: Sunnah for those intending Udhiyah/Qurbani

events.title: Day of Tarwiyah (Hajj Begins)
events.shortStory: The 8th of Dhul-Hijjah marks the official beginning of the Hajj rites, when pilgrims move to Mina and prepare for the standing at Arafah. The day is named Tarwiyah because pilgrims would gather water for the days ahead. Pilgrims enter the state of Ihram and follow the footsteps of Ibrahim (AS), embodying his family's surrender to Allah's call. For non-pilgrims, it opens the most beloved ten days to Allah — a season of fasting, takbeer, and remembrance.

quranRefs.text: And proclaim to the people the Hajj; they will come to you on foot and on every lean camel; they will come from every distant pass.
That they may witness benefits for themselves and mention the name of Allah on known days over what He has provided for them of sacrificial animals. So eat of them and feed the miserable and poor.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ said: No good deeds done on other days are superior to those done on these first ten days of Dhul-Hijjah. They asked: Not even Jihad for Allah's cause? He said: Not even Jihad, except a man who goes out himself with his wealth and does not return with either.
The Prophet ﷺ said: The most beloved of places to Allah are the mosques, and the most hated of places to Allah are the markets.

duas.arabic: لَبَّيْكَ اللَّهُمَّ لَبَّيْكَ، لَبَّيْكَ لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ لَبَّيْكَ، إِنَّ الْحَمْدَ وَالنِّعْمَةَ لَكَ وَالْمُلْكَ، لَا شَرِيكَ لَكَ
duas.transliteration: Labbayka Allahumma labbayk, labbayka la sharika laka labbayk, innal-hamda wan-ni'mata laka wal-mulk, la sharika lak
duas.translation: Here I am, O Allah, here I am. Here I am, You have no partner, here I am. Indeed all praise, bounty, and dominion are Yours, You have no partner.

duas.arabic: رَبِّ اجْعَلْنِي مُقِيمَ الصَّلَاةِ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّتِي ۚ رَبَّنَا وَتَقَبَّلْ دُعَاءِ
duas.transliteration: Rabbij'alni muqimas-salati wa min dhurriyyati, Rabbana wa taqabbal du'a
duas.translation: My Lord, make me an establisher of prayer and of my descendants. Our Lord, and accept my supplication.

sunnahActions.title: Make dua for the pilgrims
sunnahActions.subtitle: Supplicate for those beginning their Hajj journey

sunnahActions.title: Fast if not on Hajj
sunnahActions.subtitle: Continue fasting the first ten days

sunnahActions.title: Reflect on Ibrahim's submission
sunnahActions.subtitle: His Hajj was the answer to Allah's command

sunnahActions.title: Recite Takbeer abundantly
sunnahActions.subtitle: Glorify Allah throughout the blessed ten days

events.title: Day of Arafah
events.shortStory: The pinnacle of Hajj, when pilgrims stand on the plain of Arafah seeking Allah's mercy. On this day Allah perfected the religion (5:3). For non-pilgrims, fasting it expiates the sins of the previous and coming year.

quranRefs.text: This day I have perfected for you your religion and completed My favor upon you and have approved for you Islam as religion.
And when you have completed your rites, remember Allah like your [previous] remembrance of your fathers or with [much] greater remembrance.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ said: There is no day on which Allah frees more people from the Fire than the Day of Arafah. He comes close and expresses His pride to the angels, saying: What do these people want?
The best of supplication is the supplication on the Day of Arafah.

duas.arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
duas.transliteration: La ilaha illallah wahdahu la sharika lah, lahul-mulku wa lahul-hamdu wa huwa ala kulli shay'in qadir
duas.translation: There is no god but Allah alone, with no partner. His is the dominion and His is the praise, and He has power over all things

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ عَفُوٌّ كَرِيمٌ تُحِبُّ الْعَفْوَ فَاعْفُ عَنِّي
duas.transliteration: Allahumma innaka 'afuwwun karimun tuhibbul-'afwa fa'fu 'anni
duas.translation: O Allah, You are Forgiving and Generous, You love to forgive, so forgive me

sunnahActions.title: Fast the Day of Arafah (non-pilgrims)
sunnahActions.subtitle: Expiates the past year and the coming year's sins

sunnahActions.title: Make abundant dua
sunnahActions.subtitle: The Prophet ﷺ said this is the best day for supplication

sunnahActions.title: Recite the Arafah dua
sunnahActions.subtitle: La ilaha illallah wahdahu... the words of the Prophet ﷺ

sunnahActions.title: Give extra charity
sunnahActions.subtitle: Join the pilgrims in devotion through generosity

events.title: Eid al-Adha
events.shortStory: The Festival of Sacrifice, commemorating Ibrahim's (AS) willingness to sacrifice his son Ismail in obedience to Allah, who ransomed him with a great ram. Pilgrims cast the Jamarat and sacrifice animals; the Ummah worldwide joins in Udhiyah.

quranRefs.text: And when he reached with him the age of exertion, he said: O my son, indeed I have seen in a dream that I must sacrifice you, so see what you think. He said: O my father, do as you are commanded. You will find me, if Allah wills, of the steadfast.
And We ransomed him with a great sacrifice.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ delivered the Khutba on the day of Nahr and said: The first thing we do on this day of ours is to pray, then we return and sacrifice. Whoever does this has followed our way.
The Prophet ﷺ said: There is no deed that Allah has made more beloved to Him than a drop of blood shed on the day of Nahr.

duas.arabic: إِنَّ صَلَاتِي وَنُسُكِي وَمَحْيَايَ وَمَمَاتِي لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ
duas.transliteration: Inna salati wa nusuki wa mahyaya wa mamati lillahi rabbil-'alamin
duas.translation: Indeed, my prayer, my sacrifice, my living, and my dying are for Allah, Lord of the worlds

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ تَقَبَّلْ مِنِّي كَمَا تَقَبَّلْتَ مِنْ خَلِيلِكَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ
duas.transliteration: Allahumma taqabbal minni kama taqabbalta min khalilika Ibrahim
duas.translation: O Allah, accept from me as You accepted from Your friend Ibrahim

sunnahActions.subtitle: Allahu Akbar after each prayer these days

events.title: Days of Tashriq
events.shortStory: The 11th, 12th, and 13th of Dhul-Hijjah are the Days of Tashriq — days of eating, drinking, and remembrance of Allah. The Prophet ﷺ said they are days of Eid and worship, not of fasting (for non-pilgrims). Pilgrims stone the Jamarat and continue their Hajj rites, while the Ummah everywhere keeps the Takbeer alive after every prayer. They are a continuation of celebration and a reminder that true joy is found in remembering Allah after His greatest acts of worship.

quranRefs.text: And remember Allah during specific numbered days. Then whoever hastens his departure in two days — there is no sin upon him; and whoever delays — there is no sin upon him — for him who fears Allah. And fear Allah and know that to Him you will be gathered.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ said: The days of Tashriq are days of eating and drinking.
The Prophet ﷺ said: The days of Mina are days of eating and drinking and remembrance of Allah.

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَعِنِّي عَلَى ذِكْرِكَ وَشُكْرِكَ وَحُسْنِ عِبَادَتِكَ
duas.transliteration: Allahumma a'inni 'ala dhikrika wa shukrika wa husni 'ibadatik
duas.translation: O Allah, help me to remember You, to thank You, and to worship You in the best manner.

duas.arabic: فَإِذَا قَضَيْتُم مَّنَاسِكَكُمْ فَاذْكُرُوا اللَّهَ كَذِكْرِكُمْ آبَاءَكُمْ أَوْ أَشَدَّ ذِكْرًا
duas.transliteration: Fa-idha qadaytum manasikakum fazkurullaha ka-dhikrikum aba'akum aw ashadda dhikra
duas.translation: And when you have completed your rites, remember Allah like your remembrance of your fathers or with much greater remembrance.

sunnahActions.title: Remember Allah abundantly
sunnahActions.subtitle: Eat, drink, and glorify Allah as the Prophet ﷺ taught

sunnahActions.title: Eat and give thanks
sunnahActions.subtitle: Enjoy lawful provision and praise Allah for His bounty

events.title: Ayyam al-Biyd (White Days)
events.shortStory: The 13th, 14th, and 15th of every Hijri month are the White Days, when the moon is full and the nights are illuminated. The Prophet ﷺ fasted these days regularly and encouraged his companions to do so, saying that fasting three days each month is equivalent to fasting a lifetime. These days offer a recurring opportunity to renew one's spiritual commitment through voluntary fasting, drawing closer to Allah through a sunnah that spans the entire year.

quranRefs.text: [Fasting for] a limited number of days. So whoever among you is ill or on a journey [during them] - then an equal number of days [are to be made up]. And upon those who are able [to fast, but with hardship] - a ransom [as substitute] of feeding a poor person [each day]. And whoever volunteers excess - it is better for him. But to fast is best for you, if you only knew.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ said to me, 'You fast daily all the year and pray every night all the night?' I replied in the affirmative. The Prophet said, 'If you keep on doing this, your eyes will become weak and your body will get tired. He who fasts the whole year has not fasted. Fast like the fasting of Dawud who used to fast on alternate days, and fasting three days (a month) will be equal to the fasting of the whole year.'

The Messenger of Allah ﷺ was asked about fasting on the day of Arafah, whereupon he said: 'It expiates the sins of the preceding year and the coming year.' And he was asked about fasting on the day of Ashura, whereupon he said: 'It expiates the sins of the preceding year.' He was asked about fasting three days of every month, whereupon he said: 'That is fasting for a lifetime.'

duas.arabic: ذَهَبَ الظَّمَأُ وَابْتَلَّتِ الْعُرُوقُ وَثَبَتَ الْأَجْرُ إِنْ شَاءَ اللَّهُ
duas.transliteration: Dhahaba adh-dhama'u, wa-btallati al-ʿuruqu, wa-thabata al-ajru in sha'a Allah
duas.translation: The thirst has gone, the veins are moistened, and the reward is confirmed, if Allah wills.

sunnahActions.title: Fast the White Days
sunnahActions.subtitle: The 13th, 14th, and 15th of this Hijri month — a lifelong sunnah

sunnahActions.title: Recite Surah Al-Mulk
sunnahActions.subtitle: The Prophet ﷺ recommended it for protection in the grave

sunnahActions.title: Make dua at iftar
sunnahActions.subtitle: The fasting person's supplication is not rejected

events.title: Month of Safar
events.shortStory: Safar is the second month of the Hijri calendar. In pre-Islamic times, people feared it as unlucky and believed it brought misfortune, but the Prophet ﷺ emphatically rejected such superstition and taught that no month, day, or bird carries evil in itself. This is a day to renew trust in Allah's decree (qadar) and reject all forms of superstition — affirming that Allah alone controls all good and harm.

quranRefs.text: No disaster strikes upon the earth or among yourselves except that it is in a register before We bring it into being - indeed that, for Allah, is easy.
Say, 'Never will we be struck except by what Allah has decreed for us; He is our protector.' And upon Allah let the believers rely.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ said, 'There is no 'adwa (no contagious disease is conveyed without Allah's permission), nor is there any bad omen (from birds), nor is there any Hamah, nor is there any bad omen in the month of Safar.'

The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, 'There is no transitive disease, no ill omen, no safar (evil omen in the month of Safar), no hama (owl), and no naw' (evil star). And flee from leprosy as you flee from a lion.'

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْهَمِّ وَالْحَزَنِ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْعَجْزِ وَالْكَسَلِ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْجُبْنِ وَالْبُخْلِ، وَأَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنْ غَلَبَةِ الدَّيْنِ وَقَهْرِ الرِّجَالِ
duas.transliteration: Allahumma inni aʿudhu bika min al-hammi wal-hazan, wa-aʿudhu bika min al-ʿajzi wal-kasal, wa-aʿudhu bika min al-jubni wal-bukhl, wa-aʿudhu bika min ghalabati ad-dayni wa-qahri ar-rijal
duas.translation: O Allah, I seek refuge in You from anxiety and sorrow, from inability and laziness, from cowardice and miserliness, and from being overpowered by debt and the oppression of men.

sunnahActions.title: Reject superstition
sunnahActions.subtitle: Affirm that Allah alone controls all good and harm — no month is unlucky

sunnahActions.title: Recite Surah Al-Fatihah 7×
sunnahActions.subtitle: Seek Allah's protection from all afflictions

sunnahActions.title: Study the hadith on qadar
sunnahActions.subtitle: Reflect on the Prophet's ﷺ teaching that no omen carries harm

events.title: Rabi al-Thani Begins
events.shortStory: The fourth month of the Hijri calendar, following the month of the Prophet's birth. It is a time to continue practicing the sunnah habits you began in Rabi al-Awwal — the Prophet ﷺ taught that the most beloved deeds to Allah are those done consistently, even if small. Use this month to audit your spiritual habits, renew your commitment to regular worship, and seek forgiveness for any shortcomings in your practice.

quranRefs.text: And those who strive for Us - We will surely guide them to Our ways. And indeed, Allah is with the doers of good.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ said, 'Do (good) deeds which are within your capacity, as Allah does not get tired (of giving rewards) but you will get tired, and the best deed in the sight of Allah is that which is done regularly.'
The Prophet ﷺ said, 'By Allah, I seek forgiveness from Allah and repent to Him more than seventy times a day.'

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ أَعِنِّي عَلَى ذِكْرِكَ وَشُكْرِكَ وَحُسْنِ عِبَادَتِكَ
duas.transliteration: Allahumma aʿinni ʿala dhikrika wa-shukrika wa-husni ʿibadatik
duas.translation: O Allah, help me to remember You, to be grateful to You, and to worship You in the best manner.

sunnahActions.title: Audit your daily habits
sunnahActions.subtitle: Check which sunnah practices you've maintained this year

sunnahActions.title: Pray Istighfar 100×
sunnahActions.subtitle: The Prophet ﷺ sought Allah's forgiveness 100 times a day

sunnahActions.title: Commit to one new daily sunnah
sunnahActions.subtitle: Consistency is the most beloved deed to Allah — start small

events.title: Jumada al-Awwal Begins
events.shortStory: The fifth month of the Hijri calendar, historically the month of frost. It is a quiet month in the Islamic year — an opportunity to strengthen daily worship, increase charity, and maintain spiritual momentum after the first third of the year. The Prophet ﷺ taught that even a small act of charity given from honest earnings is magnified by Allah, growing like a foal until it becomes as great as a mountain.

quranRefs.text: Indeed, those who believe and do righteous deeds and establish prayer and give zakah will have their reward with their Lord, and there will be no fear concerning them, nor will they grieve.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ said, 'If one gives in charity what equals one date-fruit from honestly earned money — and Allah accepts only the honestly earned money — Allah takes it in His right hand and then enlarges its reward for that person, as anyone of you brings up his baby horse, so much so that it becomes as big as a mountain.'
The Prophet ﷺ said, 'Guard yourselves from the Fire even with half a date.'

duas.arabic: رَبَّنَا آتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
duas.transliteration: Rabbana atina fid-dunya hasanatan wa fil-akhirati hasanatan wa-qina ʿadhaban-nar
duas.translation: Our Lord, give us in this world [that which is] good and in the Hereafter [that which is] good and protect us from the punishment of the Fire.

sunnahActions.title: Give regular charity (Sadaqah)
sunnahActions.subtitle: Even a smile is charity — give something today

sunnahActions.title: Recite Surah Al-Kahf
sunnahActions.subtitle: A weekly sunnah that brings light between Fridays

sunnahActions.title: Sponsor a water well or food donation
sunnahActions.subtitle: Even half a date guards you from the Fire

events.title: Battle of Mu'tah
events.shortStory: In Jumada al-Awwal of the 8th year after Hijrah, a small Muslim army of 3,000 faced a Byzantine force of 100,000+ at Mu'tah. The Prophet ﷺ appointed three commanders in succession: Zayd ibn Harithah, Ja'far ibn Abi Talib, and Abdullah ibn Rawahah — all three were martyred. Then Khalid ibn al-Walid took command and executed a brilliant tactical withdrawal, reorganizing the ranks and saving the army. The Prophet ﷺ called him 'Sword of Allah' (Sayfullah) for his valor.

quranRefs.text: O you who have believed, if you support Allah, He will support you and plant firmly your feet.

hadithRefs.text: Allah's Messenger ﷺ appointed Zaid bin Haritha as the commander of the army during the Ghazwa of Mu'tah and said, 'If Zaid is martyred, Ja'far should take over his position, and if Ja'far is martyred, Abdullah bin Rawaha should take over his position.' Ibn Umar said, 'I was present amongst them in that battle and we searched for Ja'far bin Abi Talib and found his body amongst the bodies of the martyred ones, and found over ninety wounds over his body.'

The Prophet ﷺ announced the martyrdom of Zayd, Ja'far, and Ibn Rawaha before the news reached Medina, and his eyes were shedding tears as he said, 'The flag was taken by Khalid bin Al-Walid, and he was a sword from the swords of Allah.'

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اغْفِرْ لِأَمَتِكَ فُلَانٍ وَافْسَحْ لَهَا فِي قَبْرِهَا وَنَوِّرْ لَهَا فِيهِ
duas.transliteration: Allahumma-ghfir li-amatika fulan, wa-fsah laha fi qabriha, wa-nawwir laha fih
duas.translation: O Allah, forgive Your servant [name], and make spacious for him in his grave, and bring light to it for him.

sunnahActions.title: Remember the martyrs in your dua
sunnahActions.subtitle: Zayd, Ja'far, and Abdullah — the three commanders of Mu'tah

sunnahActions.title: Reflect on courage and faith
sunnahActions.subtitle: Few can prevail against many when Allah grants His aid

sunnahActions.title: Learn about Khalid ibn al-Walid
sunnahActions.subtitle: The 'Sword of Allah' — study his tactical wisdom and devotion

events.title: Jumada al-Thani Begins
events.shortStory: The sixth month of the Hijri calendar. Historically, Fatimah (RA), the beloved daughter of the Prophet ﷺ, married Ali ibn Abi Talib (RA) in this month. It is a reminder of the simple, faith-centered family life taught by the Prophet ﷺ, who taught that whoever desires to have their provision increased and their lifespan prolonged should maintain family ties (silat al-rahm). This month invites us to strengthen our bonds of kinship.

quranRefs.text: And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquility in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ said, 'Whoever is pleased that he be granted more wealth and that his lease of life be prolonged, then he should keep good relations with his kith and kin.'

The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, 'Verily, Allah created the creatures, and when He finished the creation, the womb got up and caught hold of Allah. Allah said, "What is the matter?" The womb said, "I seek refuge with You from Al-Qati'ah (the one who severs the ties of kinship)." Allah said, "Will you be pleased if I bestow My favors on him who keeps your ties and withhold My favors from him who severs your ties?" The womb said, "Yes, my Lord." Allah said, "Then that is for you."'

duas.arabic: رَبِّ ارْحَمْهُمَا كَمَا رَبَّيَانِي صَغِيرًا
duas.transliteration: Rabbi-rhamhuma kama rabbayani saghira
duas.translation: My Lord, have mercy upon them as they brought me up [when I was] small.

sunnahActions.title: Strengthen family ties (Silat al-Rahm)
sunnahActions.subtitle: Call or visit a family member you haven't spoken to recently

sunnahActions.title: Make dua for your parents
sunnahActions.subtitle: My Lord, have mercy upon them as they brought me up (17:24)

sunnahActions.title: Reflect on the marriage of Fatimah & Ali
sunnahActions.subtitle: Study the Prophet's ﷺ model of simple, faith-centered family life

events.title: Rajab — Sacred Month Begins
events.shortStory: Rajab is one of the four sacred months in which fighting is forbidden. The Prophet ﷺ would say: 'O Allah, bless us in Rajab and Sha'ban, and let us reach Ramadan.' It is a month to prepare the heart for the arrival of Ramadan. As the first of the three consecutive sacred months leading to Ramadan, it opens a season of spiritual renewal, voluntary worship, and introspection. Honoring Rajab means guarding the tongue, increasing repentance, and beginning the inward journey toward the Quran.

quranRefs.text: Indeed, the number of months with Allah is twelve months in the register of Allah from the day He created the heavens and the earth; of these, four are sacred. That is the correct religion, so do not wrong yourselves during them.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ would say when Rajab began: O Allah, bless us in Rajab and Sha'ban, and let us reach Ramadan.
The Prophet ﷺ said: The year is twelve months, of which four are sacred: Dhul-Qa'dah, Dhul-Hijjah, Muharram, and Rajab of Mudar, which is between Jumada and Sha'ban.

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ بَارِكْ لَنَا فِي رَجَبَ وَشَعْبَانَ وَبَلِّغْنَا رَمَضَانَ
duas.transliteration: Allahumma barik lana fi Rajab wa Sha'ban wa ballighna Ramadan
duas.translation: O Allah, bless us in Rajab and Sha'ban, and let us reach Ramadan.

duas.arabic: رَبِّ اشْرَحْ لِي صَدْرِي وَيَسِّرْ لِي أَمْرِي وَاحْلُلْ عُقْدَةً مِّن لِّسَانِي يَفْقَهُوا قَوْلِي
duas.transliteration: Rabbish-rah li sadri wa yassir li amri wahlul 'uqdatam min lisani yafqahu qawli
duas.translation: My Lord, expand for me my breast and ease for me my task and untie the knot from my tongue that they may understand my speech.

sunnahActions.title: Recite the Rajab dua
sunnahActions.subtitle: Allahumma barik lana fi Rajab wa Sha'ban wa ballighna Ramadan

sunnahActions.title: Fast voluntarily in Rajab
sunnahActions.subtitle: Increase voluntary fasting in this sacred month

sunnahActions.title: Begin Quran review
sunnahActions.subtitle: Prepare for Ramadan by building a daily recitation habit now

sunnahActions.title: Guard your tongue
sunnahActions.subtitle: Wrongdoing in sacred months is graver — speak only good

events.title: Expedition of Tabuk
events.shortStory: In Rajab of the 9th year after Hijrah, the Prophet ﷺ marched with 30,000 companions to Tabuk to confront the Byzantine army. Though no battle occurred, it was a severe test of faith in the scorching desert heat — and several hypocrites made excuses to avoid going. The Quran rebuked those who were reluctant to strive with their wealth and lives in Allah's cause, while praising those who willingly sacrificed for the faith.

quranRefs.text: O you who have believed, what is [the matter] with you that, when you are told to go forth in the cause of Allah, you adhere heavily to the earth? Are you satisfied with the life of this world rather than the Hereafter? But what is the enjoyment of worldly life compared to the Hereafter except a [very] little.

Had it been an easy gain and a moderate trip, they would have followed you, but distant was the distance for them. And they will swear by Allah, 'If we were able, we would have gone forth with you,' destroying themselves [through falsehood]. And Allah knows that they are liars.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ was asked, 'Who is the best among the people?' He replied, 'A believer who strives his utmost in Allah's Cause with his life and property.' They asked, 'Who is next?' He replied, 'A believer who stays in one of the mountain paths worshipping Allah and leaving the people secure from his mischief.'

The Prophet ﷺ dictated: 'Not equal are those of the believers who sit (at home) and those who strive and fight in the Cause of Allah.' Then Ibn Umm Maktum, who was blind, came and said, 'O Allah's Apostle! By Allah, if I had the power to fight, I would.' So Allah revealed: 'Except those who are disabled.'

duas.arabic: رَبَّنَا أَفْرِغْ عَلَيْنَا صَبْرًا وَثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَنَا وَانْصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ
duas.transliteration: Rabbana afrigh ʿalayna sabran wa-thabbit aqdamana wa-nsurna ʿala al-qawmi al-kafirin
duas.translation: Our Lord, pour upon us patience and plant firmly our feet and give us victory over the disbelieving people.

sunnahActions.title: Reflect on sacrifice for faith
sunnahActions.subtitle: The companions gave wealth and endured hardship willingly for Allah's cause

sunnahActions.title: Read Surah At-Tawbah 38-42
sunnahActions.subtitle: Allah tests who will strive with wealth and lives

sunnahActions.title: Support those striving in Allah's cause
sunnahActions.subtitle: Contribute to causes that serve the Ummah — wealth or effort

events.title: Dhu al-Qidah — Sacred Month
events.shortStory: Dhu al-Qidah is one of the four sacred months in which fighting is forbidden. The Prophet ﷺ taught that three sacred months are in succession — Dhu al-Qidah, Dhu al-Hijjah, and Muharram — and the fourth is Rajab. The Treaty of Hudaybiyyah was concluded in this month, and the Prophet ﷺ performed Umrah during it, demonstrating that the sacred months are a time of peace, pilgrimage, and reconciliation rather than conflict.

quranRefs.text: [Fighting in] the sacred month is for [aggression committed in] the sacred month, and for [all] violations is legal retribution. So whoever has assaulted you, then assault him in the same way that he has assaulted you. And fear Allah and know that Allah is with those who fear Him.

Indeed, the number of months with Allah is twelve [lunar] months in the register of Allah [from] the day He created the heavens and the earth; of these, four are sacred. That is the correct religion, so do not wrong yourselves during them. And fight against the disbelievers collectively as they fight against you collectively. And know that Allah is with the righteous.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ said, 'The year is of twelve months, out of which four months are sacred: Three are in succession — Dhu al-Qa'da, Dhu al-Hijja and Muharram — and (the fourth is) Rajab of (the tribe of) Mudar which comes between Jumada-ath-Thaniyah and Sha'ban.'

The Prophet ﷺ set out at the time of Al-Hudaibiya and said, 'We have not come to fight anyone, but to perform Umrah. No doubt, the war has weakened Quraish and they have suffered great losses, so if they wish, I will conclude a truce with them.' This became the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah, concluded in the sacred month of Dhu al-Qidah.

duas.arabic: رَبَّنَا تَقَبَّلْ مِنَّا إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
duas.transliteration: Rabbana taqabbal minna, innaka anta as-samiʿu al-ʿalim
duas.translation: Our Lord, accept it from us. Indeed, You are the Hearing, the Knowing.

sunnahActions.title: Reflect on peace and truce
sunnahActions.subtitle: The sacred months are for reconciliation and worship, not conflict

sunnahActions.title: Reconcile a broken relationship
sunnahActions.subtitle: Hudaybiyyah teaches that peace can be a greater victory than war

sunnahActions.title: Prepare for Umrah or Hajj
sunnahActions.subtitle: Dhu al-Qidah is the month the Prophet ﷺ performed Umrah

events.title: Conquest of Makkah
events.shortStory: On the 20th of Ramadan in the 8th year after Hijrah, the Prophet ﷺ returned to Makkah with 10,000 companions and conquered it almost without bloodshed. He forgave his enemies with the words of Yusuf (AS): 'No blame will there be upon you today. May Allah forgive you, and He is the Most Merciful of the merciful.' He cleansed the Ka'bah of 360 idols, restoring the House of Ibrahim (AS) to pure monotheism. The conquest was not of land but of hearts — mercy triumphed over vengeance, and the city that once expelled him now embraced him.

quranRefs.text: When the victory of Allah has come and the conquest.
And you see the people entering into the religion of Allah in multitudes.

hadithRefs.text: On the day of the conquest of Makkah, the Prophet ﷺ said: No reproach this day. O Allah, forgive the people of the Quraysh.
The Prophet ﷺ entered Makkah on the day of its conquest wearing a helmet, and when he removed it a man came and said: Ibn Khatal is clinging to the covering of the Ka'bah. He said: Kill him.

duas.arabic: لَا تَثْرِيبَ عَلَيْكُمُ الْيَوْمَ ۖ يَغْفِرُ اللَّهُ لَكُمْ ۖ وَهُوَ أَرْحَمُ الرَّاحِمِينَ
duas.transliteration: La tathriba 'alaykumul-yawm, yaghfirullahu lakum, wa huwa arhamur-rahimin
duas.translation: No blame will there be upon you today. Allah will forgive you, and He is the Most Merciful of the merciful.

duas.arabic: رَبَّنَا اغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَإِسْرَافَنَا فِي أَمْرِنَا وَثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَنَا
duas.transliteration: Rabbana-ghfir lana dhunubana wa israfana fi amrina wa thabbit aqdamana
duas.translation: Our Lord, forgive us our sins and our transgression and make our feet firm.

sunnahActions.title: Forgive someone today
sunnahActions.subtitle: Emulate the Prophet's forgiveness of his worst enemies

sunnahActions.title: Read Surah An-Nasr
sunnahActions.subtitle: The chapter revealed prophesying this conquest

sunnahActions.title: Make dua for guidance
sunnahActions.subtitle: As hearts entered Islam in multitudes, pray for the Ummah today

sunnahActions.title: Cleanse the heart of idols
sunnahActions.subtitle: Remove hidden attachments just as the Ka'bah was cleansed of idols

events.title: Day of Badr
events.shortStory: On the 17th of Ramadan in the 2nd year after Hijrah, 313 Muslims led by the Prophet ﷺ defeated a Makkan army of nearly 1,000 at Badr. Allah sent angels to aid the believers, and the Quran calls the day of Badr the Day of Criterion (Furqan) — separating truth from falsehood. It was the turning point that established the Muslim community in Arabia, proving that victory lies not in numbers but in Allah's support. The believers fasted and prayed through the night before the battle, and at dawn Allah granted them a decisive triumph.

quranRefs.text: When you sought help from your Lord, and He answered you: Indeed, I will reinforce you with a thousand of the angels, following one another.

And you did not kill them, but it was Allah who killed them. And you threw not when you threw, but it was Allah who threw, that He might test the believers with a good test. And indeed, Allah is Hearing and Knowing.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ said on the day of Badr: This is Jibril holding the head of his horse and equipped for war.

The Prophet ﷺ said: Five are of the righteous martyrs: the one who dies of plague, the one who dies of a stomach disease, the one who drowns, the one who is crushed, and the one martyred in the path of Allah.

duas.arabic: حَسْبُنَا اللَّهُ وَنِعْمَ الْوَكِيلُ
duas.transliteration: Hasbunallahu wa ni'mal-wakil
duas.translation: Sufficient for us is Allah, and the best Disposer of affairs.

duas.arabic: رَبَّنَا أَفْرِغْ عَلَيْنَا صَبْرًا وَثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَنَا وَانصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ
duas.transliteration: Rabbana afrigh 'alayna sabran wa thabbit aqdamana wansurna 'alal-qawmil-kafirin
duas.translation: Our Lord, pour upon us patience and plant firmly our feet and give us victory over the disbelieving people.

sunnahActions.title: Reflect on Allah's help
sunnahActions.subtitle: Victory comes from Allah, not from numbers or strength

sunnahActions.title: Read Surah Al-Anfal
sunnahActions.subtitle: Allah's own account of the Battle of Badr

sunnahActions.title: Fast and remember the night of prayer
sunnahActions.subtitle: The believers fasted and prayed the night before victory

sunnahActions.title: Steadfastness in truth
sunnahActions.subtitle: Stand firm for justice even when outnumbered, trusting Allah's aid

events.title: Mondays & Thursdays — Fast of the Prophet ﷺ
events.shortStory: The Prophet ﷺ fasted on Mondays and Thursdays, saying: 'The deeds of people are presented to Allah on Monday and Thursday, and I like my deeds to be presented while I am fasting.' These are days of increased dhikr and spiritual reflection, offering a weekly rhythm of voluntary worship. The Prophet ﷺ was also born on a Monday, adding to the spiritual significance of this day.

quranRefs.text: [Fasting for] a limited number of days. So whoever among you is ill or on a journey [during them] - then an equal number of days [are to be made up]. And upon those who are able [to fast, but with hardship] - a ransom [as substitute] of feeding a poor person [each day]. And whoever volunteers excess - it is better for him. But to fast is best for you, if you only knew.

hadithRefs.text: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, 'The deeds of people are presented (to Allah) on Mondays and Thursdays, and I love that my deeds be presented while I am fasting.'
The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said, 'The most excellent fast after Ramadan is God's month, al-Muharram, and the most excellent prayer after what is prescribed is prayer during the night.'

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اجْعَلْ صِيَامِي فِيهِ صِيَامَ الصَّائِمِينَ، وَقِيَامِي فِيهِ قِيَامَ الْقَائِمِينَ، وَنَبِّهْنِي فِيهِ عَنْ نَوْمَةِ الْغَافِلِينَ
duas.transliteration: Allahumma-jʿal siyami fihi siyam as-sa'imina, wa-qiyami fihi qiyam al-qaimina, wa-nabbihni fihi ʿan nawmati al-ghafilin
duas.translation: O Allah, make my fasting in it the fasting of those who fast, and my standing in it the standing of those who stand, and awaken me in it from the sleep of the heedless.

sunnahActions.title: Fast today (if Monday or Thursday)
sunnahActions.subtitle: The Prophet's weekly sunnah fasting — deeds are presented while fasting

sunnahActions.title: Increase your dhikr
sunnahActions.subtitle: Subhanallah, Alhamdulillah, Allahu Akbar — 33× each

sunnahActions.title: Make dua for accepted worship
sunnahActions.subtitle: Ask Allah to accept your fasting and righteous deeds

events.title: Wafat of the Prophet ﷺ
events.shortStory: On 12 Rabi al-Awwal 11 AH, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ returned to his Lord, his head resting between the shoulders of Aisha (RA) in her chamber in Madinah. He had just completed the religion, and his Ummah was left with the Book of Allah ﷺ and his Sunnah. It is a day of immense grief for his passing and immense gratitude for the gift of his prophethood.

quranRefs.text: Muhammad is not but a messenger. Other messengers have passed on before him. So if he were to die or be killed, would you turn back on your heels? And whoever turns back on his heels will never harm Allah at all; but Allah will reward the grateful.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ said during his final illness: O Allah, the Highest Companion. Then he raised his hand and said: In the Highest Companion — and then he passed away.
The Prophet ﷺ used to say in his illness: May Allah curse the Jews and Christians, for they took the graves of their prophets as places of worship. He was warning against what they did.

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ الرَّفِيقَ الْأَعْلَى
duas.transliteration: Allahummar-rafiqal-a'la
duas.translation: O Allah, the Highest Companion

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَعَلَى آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ كَمَا صَلَّيْتَ عَلَى إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَعَلَى آلِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ
duas.transliteration: Allahumma salli ala Muhammad wa ala aali Muhammad kama sallayta ala Ibrahim wa ala aali Ibrahim
duas.translation: O Allah, send blessings upon Muhammad and the family of Muhammad, as You sent blessings upon Ibrahim and the family of Ibrahim

sunnahActions.title: Send abundant salawat
sunnahActions.subtitle: Send salutations upon the Prophet ﷺ

sunnahActions.title: Increase durood
sunnahActions.subtitle: Recite durood shareef abundantly

sunnahActions.title: Reflect on his legacy
sunnahActions.subtitle: Study his ﷺ final advice

events.title: Laylat al-Qadr (29th Night)
events.shortStory: The Night of Decree is better than a thousand months, when the angels and the Spirit descend by permission of their Lord. The Prophet ﷺ commended seeking it in the odd last nights of Ramadan (21st, 23rd, 25th, 27th, and 29th). Worship on this single night surpasses the reward of worship in over 83 years.

quranRefs.text: The Night of Decree is better than a thousand months.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ said: Seek Laylat al-Qadr in the odd nights of the last ten days of Ramadan.
I said: O Messenger of Allah, if I know which night is Laylat al-Qadr, what should I say? He said: Say: Allahumma innaka 'afuwwun tuhibbul-'afwa fa'fu 'anni.

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ عَفُوٌّ تُحِبُّ الْعَفْوَ فَاعْفُ عَنِّي
duas.transliteration: Allahumma innaka 'afuwwun tuhibbul-'afwa fa'fu 'anni
duas.translation: O Allah, You are the Most Forgiving and You love to forgive, so forgive me

duas.arabic: رَبِّ قَدْ آتَيْتَنِي مِنَ الْمُلْكِ وَعَلَّمْتَنِي مِن تَأْوِيلِ الْأَحَادِيثِ
duas.transliteration: Rabbi qad ataytani minal-mulki wa 'allamtani min ta'wilil-ahadith
duas.translation: My Lord, You have given me sovereignty and taught me the interpretation of hadiths

sunnahActions.title: Stand the night in prayer
sunnahActions.subtitle: Pray with faith and hope of reward

sunnahActions.title: Recite the Qadr dua
sunnahActions.subtitle: Allahumma innaka 'afuwwun...

sunnahActions.title: Recite Quran
sunnahActions.subtitle: Spend time with the Quran

events.title: Battle of Uhud
events.shortStory: On 7 Shawwal 3 AH, the Muslims met the Quraysh at the foot of Mount Uhud. Despite early victory, a moment of disobedience by the archers turned the day into a trial in which the Prophet ﷺ himself was wounded and 70 Companions were martyred. The battle taught the Ummah that victory lies in obedience to Allah and His Messenger ﷺ.

quranRefs.text: And Allah had certainly fulfilled His promise to you when you were killing the enemy by His permission, until when you lost courage and fell to disputing about the order and disobeyed after He had shown you that which you love...

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ said: The foremost to enter Paradise will be the people of Badr, and the people of Uhud are not like the people of Badr.
The Prophet ﷺ said: By Him in Whose Hand is my soul, I wish I could fight in the way of Allah and be killed, then brought back to life and killed again.

duas.arabic: رَبَّنَا اغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَإِسْرَافَنَا فِي أَمْرِنَا
duas.transliteration: Rabbanaghfir lana dhunubana wa israfana fi amrina
duas.translation: Our Lord, forgive us our sins and our transgressions in our affairs

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ ثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَنَا وَانْصُرْنَا عَلَى الْقَوْمِ الْكَافِرِينَ
duas.transliteration: Allahumma thabbit aqdamana wansurna alal-qawmil-kafirin
duas.translation: O Allah, make our feet firm and support us against the disbelieving people

sunnahActions.title: Reflect on lessons
sunnahActions.subtitle: Contemplate obedience to the Prophet ﷺ

sunnahActions.title: Read Surah Al Imran
sunnahActions.subtitle: Reflect on verses 121-179

sunnahActions.title: Remember the martyrs
