events.title: Battle of the Trench (Khandaq)
events.shortStory: In Shawwal 5 AH, a great confederacy of tribes laid siege to Madinah. On the counsel of Salman al-Farisi (RA), the Muslims dug a trench around the city. A bitter wind was sent against the enemy and they left in frustration. The Quran calls this a manifest victory that averted the greatest threat the young community had yet faced.

quranRefs.text: O you who have believed, remember the favor of Allah upon you when armies came to you and We sent upon them a wind and armies you did not see. And ever is Allah, of what you do, Seeing.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ dug the trench along with the Companions, and they would carry the earth on their backs, saying: By Allah, without Allah we would not have been guided.
During the battle of the trench, I was with the Prophet ﷺ on guard duty through the night.

duas.arabic: لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ صَدَقَ وَعْدَهُ وَنَصَرَ عَبْدَهُ
duas.transliteration: La ilaha illallah wahdahu, sadaqa wa'dahu wa nasara 'abdahu
duas.translation: There is no god but Allah alone. He fulfilled His promise and supported His servant

duas.arabic: حَسْبُنَا اللَّهُ وَنِعْمَ الْوَكِيلُ
duas.transliteration: Hasbunallahu wa ni'mal-wakil
duas.translation: Allah is sufficient for us, and He is the best disposer of affairs

sunnahActions.title: Reflect on tawakkul
sunnahActions.subtitle: Trust Allah in hard times

sunnahActions.title: Read Surah Al-Ahzab
sunnahActions.subtitle: Reflect on verses 9-27

sunnahActions.title: Remember the shura
sunnahActions.subtitle: Value consultation and counsel

events.title: Battle of Hunayn
events.shortStory: On ~11 Shawwal 8 AH, soon after the conquest of Makkah, the Muslims faced the tribes of Hawazin and Thaqif in the valley of Hunayn. Though vastly outnumbered, a surprise ambush initially scattered the army, yet the Prophet ﷺ stood firm on his mule and called his companions back to him. Allah granted victory and revealed that numeric strength avails nothing without reliance on Allah. The Quran recalls how numeric strength avails nothing without Allah's help — a lasting lesson that reliance belongs to Allah alone.

quranRefs.text: Certainly has Allah given you victory on many battlefields, and on the day of Hunayn, when your great number pleased you, but it did not avail you at all, and the earth was narrow for you despite its vastness, then you turned back, fleeing.

hadithRefs.text: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ sent a man from Banu Mahram at the head of an advance guard, and he was met by Haram ibn Milan, and he killed him.
The Prophet ﷺ said on the day of Hunayn: I am the Prophet, no lie; I am the son of Abd al-Muttalib, as he called his companions back to him.

duas.arabic: يَا عِبَادَ اللَّهِ أَعِينُونِي
duas.transliteration: Ya 'ibadallahi a'inuni
duas.translation: O servants of Allah, help me.

duas.arabic: رَبِّ اشْرَحْ لِي صَدْرِي وَيَسِّرْ لِي أَمْرِي وَاحْلُلْ عُقْدَةً مِّن لِّسَانِي
duas.transliteration: Rabbish-rah li sadri wa yassir li amri wahlul 'uqdatam min lisani
duas.translation: My Lord, expand for me my breast and ease for me my task and untie the knot from my tongue.

sunnahActions.title: Reflect on reliance (Tawakkul)
sunnahActions.subtitle: Numbers avail not without Allah — renew your trust in Him

sunnahActions.title: Read At-Tawbah 9:25-26
sunnahActions.subtitle: Study the lesson of Hunayn and Allah's calming of the believers

sunnahActions.title: Steadfastness in trial
sunnahActions.subtitle: Hold firm when others scatter, as the Prophet ﷺ stood firm

sunnahActions.title: Examine reliance on means
sunnahActions.subtitle: Repent from over-trusting numbers, wealth, or plans over Allah

events.title: Treaty of Hudaybiyyah
events.shortStory: In Dhu al-Qidah 6 AH, the Prophet ﷺ set out with his Companions to perform Umrah, but was barred from Makkah. At Hudaybiyyah a ten-year truce was struck. Though the terms seemed harsh, Allah called it a 'clear victory' (fath mubin) that opened hearts and led to the rapid spread of Islam across Arabia.

quranRefs.text: Indeed, We have given you a clear conquest.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ made a treaty at Hudaybiyyah with the Quraysh for ten years. When the Companions were grieved, the Prophet ﷺ called them and they pledged allegiance under the tree.
The Prophet ﷺ said: No one who pledged allegiance under the tree will enter the Fire.

duas.arabic: رَبَّنَا أَفْرِغْ عَلَيْنَا صَبْرًا وَثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَنَا
duas.transliteration: Rabbana afrigh 'alayna sabran wa thabbit aqdamana
duas.translation: Our Lord, pour upon us patience and plant firmly our feet

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ مِنَ الْهَمِّ وَالْحَزَنِ وَالْعَجْزِ وَالْكَسَلِ
duas.transliteration: Allahumma inni a'udhu bika minal-hammi wal-hazani wal-'ajzi wal-kasali
duas.translation: O Allah, I seek refuge in You from worry, grief, incapacity, and laziness

sunnahActions.title: Value peace
sunnahActions.subtitle: Reflect on patience and strategy

sunnahActions.title: Read Surah Al-Fath
sunnahActions.subtitle: Meditate on the 'clear victory'

sunnahActions.title: Honor pacts
sunnahActions.subtitle: Keep your trusts and agreements

events.title: First Revelation in the Cave of Hira
events.shortStory: In the month of Ramadan, in the Cave of Hira, the Angel Jibril (AS) appeared to the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ and commanded him to 'Read!' Thus began the descent of the Quran with the opening verses of Surah Al-Alaq. This moment transformed a man of Makkah into the final Messenger of Allah to humanity.

quranRefs.text: Read in the name of your Lord who created.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ said: The angel came to me and said: Read. I said: I cannot read. He took hold of me and pressed me until I could bear it no more, then released me and said: Read. This happened three times.
The first form of revelation was the true dream in sleep. The Prophet ﷺ did not see any dream that was not to come true like the dawn.

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ عَلِّمْنِي وَفَهِّمْنِي
duas.transliteration: Allahumma 'allimni wa fahhimni
duas.translation: O Allah, teach me and give me understanding

duas.arabic: رَبِّ زِدْنِي عِلْمًا
duas.transliteration: Rabbi zidni 'ilma
duas.translation: My Lord, increase me in knowledge

sunnahActions.title: Recite Surah Al-Alaq
sunnahActions.subtitle: Reflect on the first revelation

sunnahActions.title: Reflect on prophecy
sunnahActions.subtitle: Ponder the gift of revelation

sunnahActions.title: Increase in worship
sunnahActions.subtitle: Honor the month of revelation

events.title: Muharram — Sacred Month Begins
events.shortStory: Muharram is one of the four sacred months sanctified by Allah, in which good deeds are multiplied and wrongdoing is graver. The Prophet ﷺ called it 'the month of Allah' and described the fast of Ashura as an expiation for the past year. It opens the Hijri year with a call to renewal and reflection. As a month in which fighting is forbidden and repentance is encouraged, it sets the spiritual tone for the entire year ahead.

quranRefs.text: Indeed, the number of months with Allah is twelve months in the register of Allah from the day He created the heavens and the earth; of these, four are sacred. That is the correct religion, so do not wrong yourselves during them.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ was asked which prayer is the best after the obligatory prayers, and which fast is the best after Ramadan. He said: The best prayer after the obligatory is the prayer in the depths of the night, and the best fast after Ramadan is the fast of Allah, the month of Muharram.
The most beloved of fasting to Allah is the fasting of the Prophet Dawud, and the most beloved of fasting after Ramadan is the month of Allah, Muharram.

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ اجْعَلْهُ سَنَةً خَيْرًا مِّنْ سَنَةٍ مَّضَتْ
duas.transliteration: Allahumma-j'alhu sanatan khayram min sanatin maddat
duas.translation: O Allah, make it a year better than the year that has passed.

duas.arabic: رَبَّنَا آتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
duas.transliteration: Rabbana atina fid-dunya hasanatan wa fil-akhirati hasanatan wa qina 'adhaban-nar
duas.translation: Our Lord, give us in this world good and in the Hereafter good, and protect us from the punishment of the Fire.

sunnahActions.title: Fasting in Muharram
sunnahActions.subtitle: Best fast after Ramadan — fast Ashura and the 9th

sunnahActions.title: Make intentions for the year
sunnahActions.subtitle: Begin the new year with dua and spiritual goals

sunnahActions.title: Reflect on the sacred
sunnahActions.subtitle: Honor what Allah honored — learn why these months are sacred

sunnahActions.title: Increase good deeds
sunnahActions.subtitle: Good deeds are multiplied in sacred months — give charity and dhikr

events.title: Dhul-Hijjah — Sacred Month Begins
events.shortStory: Dhul-Hijjah is one of the four sacred months and hosts the Hajj and the ten best days of the year. The Prophet ﷺ said that no days are more beloved to Allah for righteous deeds than the first ten of this month. It is a season of pilgrimage, sacrifice, remembrance, and renewal of submission to Allah. For the pilgrim it is the journey of a lifetime; for the non-pilgrim it is a season of fasting, takbeer, and charity — both drawn back to the legacy of Ibrahim (AS).

quranRefs.text: And proclaim to the people the Hajj; they will come to you on foot and on every lean camel; they will come from every distant pass.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ said: No good deeds done on other days are superior to those done on these first ten days of Dhul-Hijjah. They asked: Not even Jihad? He said: Not even Jihad, except a man who goes out with his life and wealth and returns with neither.
The Prophet ﷺ said: There are no days in which righteous deeds are more beloved to Allah than these days — meaning the ten days of Dhul-Hijjah.

duas.arabic: رَبَّنَا تَقَبَّلْ مِنَّا ۖ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
duas.transliteration: Rabbana taqabbal minna innaka antas-Sami'ul-'Alim
duas.translation: Our Lord, accept it from us. Indeed, You are the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.

duas.arabic: رَبِّ اجْعَلْنِي مُقِيمَ الصَّلَاةِ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّتِي
duas.transliteration: Rabbij'alni muqimas-salati wa min dhurriyyati
duas.translation: My Lord, make me an establisher of prayer and of my descendants.

sunnahActions.title: Say Takbeer abundantly
sunnahActions.subtitle: Proclaim Allahu Akbar throughout the ten days

sunnahActions.title: Fast the first 9 days
sunnahActions.subtitle: Includes the Day of Arafah — the greatest day to fast

sunnahActions.title: Make abundant dua
sunnahActions.subtitle: Ask Allah in these most beloved of days

sunnahActions.title: Prepare your Qurbani
sunnahActions.subtitle: Honor the legacy of Ibrahim (AS) and feed the needy

events.title: Shaban — Month of Preparation
events.shortStory: Shaban is the month in which the Prophet ﷺ fasted most after Ramadan, as a bridge of devotion between Rajab and Ramadan. He ﷺ would say it is the month in which deeds are raised to the Lord of the worlds, and so he loved that his deeds be raised while he was fasting. It calls the believer to prepare the heart for the coming of Ramadan — finishing the Quran, repenting from sin, and training the body in fasting before the obligation arrives.

quranRefs.text: The month of Ramadan is that in which was revealed the Quran, a guidance for the people and clear proofs of guidance and criterion. So whoever sights the month, let him fast it.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ did not fast in any month more than Shaban, and he used to say: Take of the deeds as much as you can, for Allah does not grow weary until you grow weary.

The Prophet ﷺ used to fast until we said he would never break his fast, and he used to break his fast until we said he would never fast. I never saw the Prophet ﷺ fast a complete month except Ramadan, and I never saw him fast more in any month than Shaban.

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ بَارِكْ لَنَا فِي رَجَبَ وَشَعْبَانَ وَبَلِّغْنَا رَمَضَانَ
duas.transliteration: Allahumma barik lana fi Rajab wa Sha'ban wa ballighna Ramadan
duas.translation: O Allah, bless us in Rajab and Sha'ban, and let us reach Ramadan.

duas.arabic: رَبِّ زِدْنِي عِلْمًا
duas.transliteration: Rabbi zidni 'ilma
duas.translation: My Lord, increase me in knowledge.

sunnahActions.title: Prepare for Ramadan
sunnahActions.subtitle: Reflect on the approaching month and set your worship goals

sunnahActions.title: Increase voluntary fasting
sunnahActions.subtitle: Follow the Sunnah of fasting in Shaban

sunnahActions.title: Reflect on deeds raised
sunnahActions.subtitle: Keep good deeds flowing, for deeds ascend in this month

sunnahActions.title: Finish pending fasts
sunnahActions.subtitle: Complete any missed Ramadan fasts before the next arrives

events.title: Taraweeh — Night Prayers of Ramadan
events.shortStory: Taraweeh is the special night prayer of Ramadan, established by the Prophet ﷺ who led it in congregation before later praying it at home out of fear it would be made obligatory. Umar (RA) reunited the Ummah upon its congregation, and it has remained a beloved Ramadan Sunnah in which the entire Quran is recited over the blessed month. Standing shoulder to shoulder in the night, the believers taste the sweetness of prolonged recitation and the stillness of the heart before its Lord.

quranRefs.text: And from a part of the night, pray Tahajjud as an extra offering for yourself; perhaps your Lord will raise you to a praised station.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ went out in the middle of the night and prayed, and the men prayed behind him. In the morning the people spoke about it, and the second night more people gathered. In the morning they spoke again, and the people increased on the third night. The Prophet ﷺ came out and led the prayer, then he said: I feared it would be made obligatory upon you.
The Prophet ﷺ said: Whoever prays during the night of Ramadan with faith and seeking reward, all his previous sins will be forgiven.

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ عَفُوٌّ كَرِيمٌ تُحِبُّ الْعَفْوَ فَاعْفُ عَنِّي
duas.transliteration: Allahumma innaka 'afuwwun karimun tuhibbul-'afwa fa'fu 'anni
duas.translation: O Allah, You are the Pardoner, the Generous, and You love to pardon, so pardon me.

duas.arabic: رَبِّ اجْعَلْنِي مُقِيمَ الصَّلَاةِ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّتِي
duas.transliteration: Rabbij'alni muqimas-salati wa min dhurriyyati
duas.translation: My Lord, make me an establisher of prayer and of my descendants.

sunnahActions.title: Pray Taraweeh in congregation
sunnahActions.subtitle: Join the nightly congregation at the masjid

sunnahActions.title: Complete the Quran in Taraweeh
sunnahActions.subtitle: Follow the recitation attentively throughout the month

sunnahActions.title: Revive the nights of Ramadan
sunnahActions.subtitle: Stand in night devotion seeking Allah's pleasure

sunnahActions.title: Reflect between rak'ahs
sunnahActions.subtitle: Pause on the verses recited and let them soften the heart

events.title: Itikaf in the Last Ten of Ramadan
events.shortStory: Itikaf is the Sunnah of secluding oneself in the mosque for worship, which the Prophet ﷺ practiced in the last ten days and nights of Ramadan seeking Laylat al-Qadr. It is a time of cutting ties with worldly distractions to devote the heart entirely to the Quran, prayer, and remembrance of Allah. The Prophet ﷺ would pitch a small tent and withdraw completely, and his wives continued the practice after him. It is the Ummah's way of pressing pause on the world to seek the Night better than a thousand months.

quranRefs.text: And do not have relations with them as long as you are staying for worship in the mosques. These are the limits of Allah, so do not approach them. Thus does Allah make clear His verses to the people that they may become righteous.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ used to practice Itikaf in the last ten days of Ramadan until Allah took his soul, then his wives practiced Itikaf after him.
The Prophet ﷺ used to exert himself in worship during the last ten nights of Ramadan more than at any other time.

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ إِنَّكَ عَفُوٌّ كَرِيمٌ تُحِبُّ الْعَفْوَ فَاعْفُ عَنِّي
duas.transliteration: Allahumma innaka 'afuwwun karimun tuhibbul-'afwa fa'fu 'anni
duas.translation: O Allah, You are the Pardoner, the Generous, and You love to pardon, so pardon me.

duas.arabic: رَبَّنَا آتِنَا فِي الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ حَسَنَةً وَقِنَا عَذَابَ النَّارِ
duas.transliteration: Rabbana atina fid-dunya hasanatan wa fil-akhirati hasanatan wa qina 'adhaban-nar
duas.translation: Our Lord, give us in this world good and in the Hereafter good, and protect us from the punishment of the Fire.

sunnahActions.title: Observe Itikaf
sunnahActions.subtitle: Retreat in the masjid for the last ten days of Ramadan

sunnahActions.title: Seek Laylat al-Qadr
sunnahActions.subtitle: Devote the heart in worship on the odd nights of the last ten

sunnahActions.title: Immerse in the Quran
sunnahActions.subtitle: Spend extended time reading and reflecting on the Book

sunnahActions.title: Disconnect from distractions
sunnahActions.subtitle: Set aside worldly affairs to focus entirely on worship

events.title: Ghadir Khumm
events.shortStory: On the return journey from the Farewell Pilgrimage, at Ghadir Khumm on 18 Dhu al-Hijjah 10 AH, the Prophet ﷺ gathered the pilgrims and delivered a sermon affirming the bond of faith. He took the hand of Ali (RA) and declared that whoever he was the master (mawla) of, Ali was also his master, instructing the community on love, loyalty, and brotherhood. It is remembered as a moment that defined the believers' loyalty to one another after the Prophet's departure. O Allah, befriend whoever befriends him, and be an enemy to whoever takes him as an enemy — the Prophet's prayer on that day.

quranRefs.text: This day I have perfected for you your religion and completed My favor upon you and have approved for you Islam as religion.

hadithRefs.text: The Prophet ﷺ said on the day of Ghadir Khumm: To whomsoever I am a master (mawla), Ali is also his master. O Allah, befriend whoever befriends him, and be an enemy to whoever takes him as an enemy.
The Prophet ﷺ said: To whomsoever I am a master, Ali is his master. O Allah, support whoever supports him, and abandon whoever abandons him.

duas.arabic: اللَّهُمَّ وَالِ مَنْ وَالَاهُ وَعَادِ مَنْ عَادَاهُ
duas.transliteration: Allahumma wali man walahu wa 'adi man 'adah
duas.translation: O Allah, befriend whoever befriends him, and be an enemy to whoever takes him as an enemy.

duas.arabic: رَبَّنَا تَقَبَّلْ مِنَّا ۖ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ
duas.transliteration: Rabbana taqabbal minna innaka antas-Sami'ul-'Alim
duas.translation: Our Lord, accept it from us. Indeed, You are the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.

sunnahActions.title: Strengthen bonds of brotherhood
sunnahActions.subtitle: Renew loyalty and love among the believers

sunnahActions.title: Study the sermon at Ghadir Khumm
sunnahActions.subtitle: Read the full narration and its context from the Farewell Pilgrimage

sunnahActions.title: Pray for the Companions
sunnahActions.subtitle: Thank Allah for the Prophet's family and companions

sunnahActions.title: Honor the bonds of faith
sunnahActions.subtitle: Show gratitude for those who carried the religion to us

historicalEvents.title: The Hijrah — Migration to Madinah
historicalEvents.description: After 13 years of persecution in Makkah, the Prophet ﷺ migrated to Madinah. This event is so pivotal that the Islamic calendar begins from it. The Hijrah was not escape — it was the foundation of the first Islamic state, a society built on faith, justice, and brotherhood.

historicalEvents.title: Battle of Uhud
historicalEvents.description: The Muslims initially prevailed at Uhud but were defeated when archers disobeyed the Prophet's order and left their posts. 70 companions were martyred including Hamzah ibn Abd al-Muttalib (RA), uncle of the Prophet ﷺ. It was a lesson in discipline and obedience.

historicalEvents.title: Passing of Fatimah (RA)
historicalEvents.description: Fatimah (RA), the beloved daughter of the Prophet ﷺ and wife of Ali (RA), passed away six months after her father. The Prophet ﷺ said: 'She is the leader of the women of Paradise.' She was known for her night prayers and deep devotion.

historicalEvents.title: Passing of Abu Bakr as-Siddiq (RA)
historicalEvents.description: Abu Bakr (RA), the first caliph and closest companion of the Prophet ﷺ, passed away after a 15-day illness. Before his death he appointed Umar ibn al-Khattab (RA) as his successor. His final words were: 'O Allah, let me die as a Muslim and join me with the righteous.'

historicalEvents.title: Passing of Imam Malik ibn Anas
historicalEvents.description: Imam Malik ibn Anas, founder of the Maliki school of jurisprudence and author of Al-Muwatta — the earliest hadith compilation — passed away in Madinah. He spent his entire life in the city of the Prophet ﷺ, teaching from the very mosque the Prophet built.

historicalEvents.title: Battle of Mu'tah
historicalEvents.description: 3,000 Muslims faced 100,000 Byzantine and Arab allies at Mu'tah. Three commanders were martyred: Zayd ibn Harithah, Ja'far ibn Abi Talib, and Abdullah ibn Rawahah. Khalid ibn al-Walid took command and saved the army, earning the title 'Sword of Allah'.

historicalEvents.title: Isra and Mi'raj — The Night Journey
historicalEvents.description: One year before the Hijrah, Allah took His Prophet ﷺ in a single night from Makkah to Jerusalem (Isra) and then through the seven heavens (Mi'raj). There he met earlier prophets and received the gift of the five daily prayers.

historicalEvents.title: Battle of the Trench (Khandaq)
historicalEvents.description: A confederate army of 10,000 besieged Madinah. On Salman al-Farisi's advice, the Muslims dug a trench around the city. The siege lasted weeks and ended when Allah sent a storm that scattered the enemy.

historicalEvents.title: Liberation of Jerusalem (Bayt al-Maqdis)
historicalEvents.description: Under Caliph Umar (RA), the Muslims liberated Jerusalem from Byzantine rule without bloodshed. Umar entered the city on foot, signed a treaty of peace with its Christian inhabitants, and cleared the site of Masjid al-Aqsa to build a mosque.

historicalEvents.title: Crossing into Al-Andalus (Spain)
historicalEvents.description: Tariq ibn Ziyad, under the Umayyad Caliphate, crossed the strait (later named Jabal Tariq → Gibraltar) and began the liberation of Spain. This led to nearly 800 years of Islamic civilization in Europe — preserving and advancing knowledge during Europe's Dark Ages.

historicalEvents.title: First Revelation of the Quran
historicalEvents.description: In the Cave of Hira during the last ten nights of Ramadan, Angel Jibril appeared to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ and said 'Read!' The first verses of Surah Al-Alaq were revealed: 'Read in the name of your Lord who created' (96:1). This began 23 years of revelation.

historicalEvents.title: Battle of Badr
historicalEvents.description: 313 Muslims led by the Prophet ﷺ defeated nearly 1,000 Makkan warriors at the wells of Badr. Allah sent 1,000 angels to aid the believers. It was the first major military victory for Islam and a turning point in history.

historicalEvents.title: Compilation of the Quran under Uthman (RA)
historicalEvents.description: Concerned by different recitations across the expanding empire, Caliph Uthman (RA) ordered a standardized master copy of the Quran. Copies were sent to every major city. This unified manuscript is the basis of every Quran read today.

historicalEvents.title: Conquest of Makkah (Fath Makkah)
historicalEvents.description: After the Quraysh broke the Hudaybiyyah treaty, the Prophet ﷺ marched on Makkah with 10,000 men. He entered almost without bloodshed, destroyed 360 idols in the Ka'bah, and forgave his enemies: 'No blame on you today.'

historicalEvents.title: Passing of Ali ibn Abi Talib (RA)
historicalEvents.description: Ali (RA), the fourth caliph, cousin and son-in-law of the Prophet ﷺ, was struck by a poisoned sword while leading Fajr prayer in Kufa. He passed away two days later. Known for his knowledge and courage, the Prophet ﷺ said: 'I am the city of knowledge and Ali is its gate.'

historicalEvents.title: Battle of Hunayn
historicalEvents.description: Right after the conquest of Makkah, the tribes of Hawazin and Thaqif attacked. Though the Muslims were 12,000, they nearly lost when they felt overconfident in their numbers. Allah sent tranquility (Sakinah) and the Muslims prevailed.

historicalEvents.title: Passing of Imam al-Bukhari
historicalEvents.description: Muhammad ibn Isma'il al-Bukhari, compiler of Sahih al-Bukhari — the most authentic book after the Quran — passed away in Samarkand on the night of Eid al-Fitr. He memorized over 600,000 hadith and verified each of the 7,564 in his collection.

historicalEvents.title: Treaty of Hudaybiyyah
historicalEvents.description: The Prophet ﷺ and 1,400 companions set out for Umrah but were stopped at Hudaybiyyah. A 10-year peace treaty was signed with Makkah. Though seemingly unfavorable, Allah called it a 'clear victory' (Fath Mubin) in Surah Al-Fath.

historicalEvents.title: The Farewell Pilgrimage (Hajjat al-Wada)
historicalEvents.description: The Prophet ﷺ performed his only Hajj with over 100,000 companions. On the Day of Arafah he delivered the Farewell Sermon on Jabal al-Rahmah, declaring human brotherhood, women's rights, and the completion of the religion (5:3).

historicalEvents.title: Passing of Umar ibn al-Khattab (RA)
historicalEvents.description: Umar (RA), the second caliph under whose reign Islam spread from Persia to Egypt, was assassinated by a Persian slave while leading Fajr prayer. His 10-year reign shaped Islamic civilization. His final advice was to hold fast to the Quran.

historicalEvents.title: Passing of Uthman ibn Affan (RA)
historicalEvents.description: Uthman (RA), the third caliph who unified the Quran into a single manuscript, was martyred while reading the Quran in his home during a siege. He was 82. The Prophet ﷺ had said of him: 'Would it not please you to be counted among the martyrs?'

historicalEvents.title: Birth of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ
historicalEvents.description: The final Messenger of Allah, Muhammad ﷺ, was born in Makkah in the Year of the Elephant, traditionally commemorated on 12 Rabi al-Awwal. His birth was a mercy to the worlds, and the pre-Islamic world awaited his coming through many prophecies.

historicalEvents.title: Wafat (Passing) of the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ
historicalEvents.description: On Monday, 12 Rabi al-Awwal 11 AH, the Prophet ﷺ passed away in Madinah at the age of 63, his noble head resting between the shoulders of Aisha (RA). With his passing the era of revelation closed, and the Ummah was entrusted with the Quran and his Sunnah as its lasting guides.

historicalEvents.title: Marriage of the Prophet ﷺ to Khadijah (RA)
historicalEvents.description: Khadijah bint Khuwaylid (RA), a noble and wealthy widow of Quraysh, proposed to the honest and trustworthy Muhammad ﷺ. Their marriage, around 595 CE, became a paragon of love, loyalty, and support, lasting 25 years. She was the first to believe in his prophethood and was called the best of women of her time.

historicalEvents.title: Death of Khadijah (RA) — Year of Sorrow
historicalEvents.description: Khadijah (RA), the beloved wife of the Prophet ﷺ and Mother of the Believers, passed away in the month of Ramadan during the Year of Sorrow (c. 619 CE), shortly before the Hijrah. Her loss, together with that of Abu Talib in the same year, left the Prophet ﷺ bereft of his two greatest worldly supports.

historicalEvents.title: Death of Abu Talib
historicalEvents.description: Abu Talib, the uncle and lifelong protector of the Prophet ﷺ, passed away in the Year of Sorrow (c. 619 CE). For 40 years he shielded his nephew from the Quraysh. His loss weakened the Prophet's ﷺ position in Makkah and set the stage for the Hijrah to Madinah.

historicalEvents.title: Birth of Ali ibn Abi Talib (RA)
historicalEvents.description: Ali ibn Abi Talib (RA), cousin and son-in-law of the Prophet ﷺ, was the first youth to accept Islam. A noble tradition, widely reported, holds that he was born inside the Ka'bah, a singular honor. He became the fourth Rightly-Guided Caliph, renowned for courage and knowledge.

historicalEvents.title: Death of Imam Abu Hanifa
historicalEvents.description: Imam Abu Hanifa al-Numan ibn Thabit (d. 150 AH), founder of the Hanafi school of jurisprudence, was a leading scholar of Kufa known for his deep use of rational deduction (ra'y). His school became the most widely followed in the Muslim world. He devoted his life to teaching and died in prison rather than accept a judgeship from the Abbasids.

historicalEvents.title: Death of Imam al-Shafi'i
historicalEvents.description: Imam Muhammad ibn Idris al-Shafi'i (d. 30 Rajab 204 AH) was the founder of the Shafi'i school and a pioneer of Islamic legal theory (usul al-fiqh). His work al-Risala laid the foundations for the science of jurisprudence. He traveled widely to learn from Imam Malik in Madinah and others, combining both the schools of hadith and reason.

historicalEvents.title: Death of Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal
historicalEvents.description: Imam Ahmad ibn Hanbal (d. 12 Rabi al-Awwal 241 AH), founder of the Hanbali school, is celebrated as 'Imam of Ahl al-Sunnah' for his steadfastness during the Mihna (Inquisition). He endured imprisonment and flogging rather than declare the Quran created. His monumental compilation, the Musnad, contains over 27,000 hadith.

historicalEvents.title: Death of Imam Muslim ibn al-Hajjaj
historicalEvents.description: Imam Muslim ibn al-Hajjaj al-Naysaburi (d. 25 Rajab 261 AH) was the author of Sahih Muslim, one of the two most authentic hadith collections. His Sahih contains about 7,500 hadith selected from a pool of 300,000. He was a devoted student of Imam al-Bukhari and a master of hadith science.

historicalEvents.title: Death of Imam al-Tirmidhi
historicalEvents.description: Imam Muhammad ibn Isa al-Tirmidhi (d. 13 Rajab 279 AH) authored the Jami al-Tirmidhi, one of the six canonical hadith collections. A student of Imam al-Bukhari, he was famed for his expertise in grading hadith and his book of Shama'il depicting the noble character of the Prophet ﷺ.

historicalEvents.title: Compilation of the Quran under Abu Bakr (RA)
historicalEvents.description: After the Battle of Yamama claimed many reciters, Abu Bakr (RA), on the advice of Umar (RA), commissioned Zayd ibn Thabit (RA) to gather the Quran into a single written volume. This compilation, completed around 12-13 AH, preserved the Book of Allah for all generations and became the foundation for the later standardization under Uthman (RA).

historicalEvents.title: Battle of al-Qadisiyyah
historicalEvents.description: The Battle of al-Qadisiyyah, fought around 15-16 AH under the command of Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas (RA), was the decisive engagement that opened the heart of Sassanid Persia to Islam. The Muslim victory shattered the Persian field army and led to the fall of the Sassanid capital, Ctesiphon. It stands among the most consequential battles in Islamic history.

historicalEvents.title: Conquest of Egypt
historicalEvents.description: Around 20 AH, the Muslim army led by Amr ibn al-As (RA) completed the conquest of Egypt, bringing the Nile valley into the fold of Islam. The capture of the fortress of Babylon (Cairo) and later Alexandria ended Byzantine rule in the region. Egypt would become a center of Islamic learning and agriculture for centuries.

historicalEvents.title: Battle of Yarmuk
historicalEvents.description: The Battle of Yarmuk, fought in 636 CE (c. 13-15 AH) over several days, was the decisive battle in which a modest Muslim force defeated a vast Byzantine army, opening the Levant (Syria, Palestine, Jordan) to Islam. Commanders such as Khalid ibn al-Walid (RA) displayed extraordinary strategic genius. The victory set the stage for the spread of Islam across the eastern Mediterranean.

historicalEvents.title: Battle of Nahavand — 'Victory of Victories'
historicalEvents.description: The Battle of Nahavand, fought around 21-22 AH, was the final crushing blow to the Sassanid Persian empire. The Muslims, outnumbered, were granted a decisive victory that the Companions called the 'Victory of Victories' (Fath al-Futuh). It opened Persia and the East to Islam and effectively ended centuries of Persian imperial power.

historicalEvents.title: Martyrdom of al-Husayn (RA) at Karbala
historicalEvents.description: On Ashura, 10 Muharram 61 AH, al-Husayn ibn Ali (RA), the beloved grandson of the Prophet ﷺ, was martyred with his family and companions at Karbala. He refused to pledge allegiance to Yazid and stood for truth. The tragedy of Karbala is a lasting reminder of sacrifice for justice and faith.
